domain:
In an academic institution, students enroll in various courses offered by the university. Each student is uniquely identified by a student code. An enrollment in the course requires the specific date on which it occurred for administrative purposes.

Courses are supported by Teaching Assistants (TAs), who are identified by their first and last names. A TA may assist in multiple different courses, and the specific role of a TA within a course is defined by an assignment, which describes the details and responsibilities assigned to them. Regarding courses logistics, they consist of one or more lab sections. Each section occurs at a specific location and time slot with start and end times.

questions.question:
Should responsibilities be modeled as a separate class associated with Assignment, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Responsibilities is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual responsibilities properties.
2. Responsibilities is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when responsibilities details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the responsibilities details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for responsibilities is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for responsibilities is enough.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: A single line of text for responsibilities is enough.

The domain description does not indicate that responsibilities consist of multiple parts or require detailed information to be saved separately; it simply states that each assignment has responsibilities.

questions.question:
Should the responsibilities in assignment be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use responsibilities as string attribute in assignment, which allows any text value.
2. Use responsibilities as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grading, tutoring, course preparation, office hours or lab supervision.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The responsibilities can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The responsibilities must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: The responsibilities can be any text value.

questions.question:
Should details be modeled as a separate class associated with Assignment, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Details is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual details properties.
2. Details is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when details details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the details details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for details is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: Model details as a simple representation in Assignment.

questions.question:
Should the details in assignment be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use details as string attribute in assignment, which allows any text value.
2. Use details as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grading, tutoring, course preparation, office hours or lab supervision.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The details can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The details must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 2: Enumeration details with predefined values: grading, tutoring, course preparation, office hours and lab supervision.

questions.question:
We know that each assignment has a role. When we think about role, is it just a simple piece of information, like a line of text? 
Or is role made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each role, the detail information is saved separately, so users can filter assignment by detail.
2. The role is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the role.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the role details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for role is enough.
questions.selected: Option 1: We need the role details separately.

questions.question:
In the context of a assignment, is it always association with at least one course, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. An assignment can exist without any course.
2. An assignment must have at least one course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: An assignment can exist without any course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An assignment must have at least one course.
questions.selected: Option 2: An assignment must have at least one course.

questions.question:
In the context of a course, is it always associated with at least one assignment, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A course can exist without any assignment.
2. A course must have at least one assignment.

questions.option_1: Option 1: A course can exist without any assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A course must have at least one assignment.
questions.selected: Option 1: A course can exist without any assignment.

questions.question:

Or is description made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each description, the detail information is saved separately, so users can filter ta role by detail.
2. The description is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the description.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the description details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for description is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for description is enough.

questions.question:
Does a ta role always require an assignment to exist. 
If an assignment is removed, would all the associated ta roles also need to be removed?

For example:
1. A ta role belongs to an assignment and cannot exist on its own.
2. A ta role is linked to an assignment but can exist independently of it.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A ta role always belongs to a assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A ta role is linked to assignment but can exist independently.
questions.selected: Option 2: A ta role is linked to assignment but can exist independently.

For example:

2. A ta role must have at least one assignment.

questions.option_1: Option 1: A ta role can exist without any assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A ta role must have at least one assignment.
questions.selected: Option 1: A ta role can exist without any assignment.

questions.selected: Option 2: An assignment must have at least one ta role.

questions.question:
In the context of an assignment, can there be more than one ta role involved, or is it always associated with just one?

For example:
1. An assignment is linked to a single ta role.
2. An assignment is linked to multiple ta roles.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each assignment is always linked to a single ta role.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An assignment can be linked to multiple ta roles.
questions.selected: Option 1: Each assignment is always linked to a single ta role.

Or is time slot made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each time slot, the start time and end time information is saved separately, so users can filter lab sections by start time and end time.
2. The time slot is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the time slot.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the time slot details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for time slot is enough.
questions.selected: Option 1: We need the time slot details separately.

questions.question:
The concept enrollment potentially represents a link between courses and students. How should we understand this connection?

For example:
1. An enrollment is a concept and a link that connects a course and a student. 
2. An enrollment is a concept, but it does not represent a link between a course and a student.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The enrollment is a concept and a link between a course and a student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The enrollment does not represent a link between a course and a student.
questions.selected: Option 1: The enrollment is a concept and a link between a course and a student.
